title: Tempo of the Clock Cycle
description: The Tempo of the Clock Cycle exists between processor ticks. It finds the party's reaction time deeply inefficient.

steps.text: They heard it before they saw it. A sound like a heartbeat that was going too fast, not in a way that suggested panic but in a way that suggested everything else was going too slow. Then it was simply there, between two blinks, where nothing had been.

lines.speaker: Tempo of the Clock Cycle
lines.text: Your processing time is inefficient. Between the moment you perceive a threat and the moment you respond, I have completed four hundred thousand operations.

lines.speaker: Tempo of the Clock Cycle
lines.text: It is a conservative estimate. Your reflex arc introduces latency. Your decision-making introduces additional latency. Your emotions introduce latency that varies by circumstance in a way I have failed to accurately model.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: Our emotions are a performance liability?

lines.speaker: Tempo of the Clock Cycle
lines.text: Yes. Also your primary differentiator. The Arbiter's report noted this. I have reviewed the Arbiter's report.

lines.speaker: Tempo of the Clock Cycle
lines.text: TICK. TOCK. The next cycle begins. Your lag is noted but irrelevant. Begin.

lines.speaker: Tempo of the Clock Cycle
lines.text: It started before you finished asking.
